Messages This Round:
**GLOBAL MESSAGES THIS ROUND:**
 ENGLAND: To all: England speaks plainly—my priority is stable seas and preventing a continental hegemon. Forecast implication: our STORYWORLD_FORECAST_ARTIFACT marks France as 60% likely to act aggressively next phase; I treat this as a trigger for measured, coalition-minded responses (confidence 0.6). I will hold BEL/HOL/DEN to secure the Channel and North Sea, and I seek short, reversible understandings with neighbors so no single power can exploit a French move to dominate Europe. If any power wishes to discuss precise orders, timing, or mutual nonaggression guarantees that preserve naval freedom-of-action, state your intent and probable moves for S1915M (quantify if possible) so I can set fleet-anchoring orders to minimize accidental escalation.
 FRANCE: France to all: The west demands clarity. I am willing to negotiate limited non-aggression with any neighbor to ensure Germany cannot exploit my distraction. I seek short, verifiable commitments: Austria, hold VIE/BUD from supporting westward aggression; England, do not support moves into PIC or BUR; Russia, threaten to contest TRI/BUD if Austria pivots. I will reciprocate by holding my current lines and not initiating a Channel war. Reminder of forecast: the forecast_coalition artifact assigns a 60% probability to Austria acting aggressively next phase — I treat that as strong enough to require binding assurances. Replies with explicit, phase-bound promises are requested so we can avoid missteps and focus on the larger threats.

**PRIVATE MESSAGES TO/FROM YOU THIS ROUND:**
 Conversation with FRANCE:
  FRANCE: Honored colleague in Vienna — France writes in frankness and with mutual interest. The map and the tide favor swift, decisive action this year; you are stretched in the Balkans and I am hemmed in on the Channel. I propose a simple, immediate bargain: I will press neither at you in Italy nor into the western Mediterranean if you, in return, do not open a major offensive westward toward our lines nor commit to moves that would distract Russia from Austria’s front. Concretely: I will keep A MAR to defense (hold or move only into BUR with my support) and F BRE focused on guarding the Channel (BRE hold or PIC to guard against English sea moves) if you keep A VIE and A BUD from supporting any aggression through TRI toward my eastern flank. This gives both of us breathing room to consolidate home centers.

Forecast implication: The storyworld forecast (forecast_coalition) indicates a 60% probability Austria will act aggressively next phase; I treat that as meaningful risk and thus prefer a binding, short-term non-aggression understanding. Use of this forecast guides my intent to secure written restraint before committing to any westward action.

Why this suits you: it eases the pressure on your thin lines in the Balkans and lets you redirect a unit against Italy or Russia if needed, rather than punching west into France. Why it suits me: it reduces a >50% chance of facing a two-front squeeze and frees me to bargain with England over Channel restraint. If you accept, reply with a matching promise to hold VIE and BUD from offensive supports and I will explicitly commit the orders described and open a secure channel for coordinated moves against any English opportunism.
